LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MASS MOVEMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. (SEE MINI MINUTES AND ORDER FOR DETAILS OF THE RULING.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS. TO PLTF'S MTN TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND DEPOSITIONS; DEFT'S REQ. FOR MONETARY AND TERMINATING SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT MASS MOVEMENT, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PLAINTIFF JOESIAH BELL'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND DEPOSITIONS IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 2, 6, 9, AND PMK DEPOSITIONS I, II AND III. DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 6. MASS MOVEMENT'S RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION 2, 6, AND 9, AND ITS RESPONSES (BOTH INITIAL AND SUPPLEMENTAL) TO ALL OF PLAINTIFF'S INTERROGATORIES WITH THE EXCEPTION OF INTERROGATORY 6, ARE DEFICIENT. PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY IS AIMED AT COLLECTING RELEVANT EVIDENCE THAT IS EITHER ADMISSIBLE OR REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. WITHIN 15 DAYS, MASS MOVEMENT SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, REQUESTS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 2, 6 AND 9, AND MASS MOVEMENT SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS IN ITS POSSESSION, CUSTODY, OR CONTROL RESPONSIVE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NUMBERS 2, 6 AND 9. WITHIN 30 DAYS, MASS MOVEMENT SHALL DESIGNATE AND PRODUCE FOR DEPOSITION AT TOOKER & ANTZ, 350 SANSOME STREET, SUITE 700, SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94104 THOSE OF ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, MANAGING AGENTS, EMPLOYEES, OR AGENTS WHO ARE MOST QUALIFIED TO TESTIFY ON ITS BEHALF, TO THE EXTENT OF ANY INFORMATION KNOWN OR REASONABLY AVAILABLE TO MASS MOVEMENT, ABOUT THOSE MATTERS IDENTIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S NOTICES OF DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT MASS MOVEMENT, INC.'S PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE I, II, AND III. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED FOR REASONS STATED ON THE RECORD. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF EXECUTED A RELEASE. EVIDENCE CITED IN DEFENDANT'S STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS, THE BELL DECLARATION AND THE BELL DEPOSITION. [PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH COMPLIES WITH CCP 437(C) BY EMAIL COPYING THE OTHER PARTY.] JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. MOTION To Compel Discovery Responses , Production Of Documents And Depositions; Memo Of P&A; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jordan A. Sussman, Esq. of Zief Diment & Glickman, Tel. 415.986.3644, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF JOESIAH BELL'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Production Of Documents And Depositions is granted as to Special Interrogatories nos. 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, Requests for Production Nos. 2, 6, 9, and PMK depositions I, II and III. Denied as to Special Interrogatory no. 6. Mass Movement's responses to Requests for Production 2, 6, and 9, and its responses (both initial and supplemental) to all of Plaintiff's interrogatories with the exception of Interrogatory 6, are deficient. Plaintiff's discovery is aimed at collecting relevant evidence that is either admissible or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Within 15 days, Mass Movement shall provide verified further responses to Special Interrogatories Nos. 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, Requests Request for Production nos. 2, 6 and 9, and Mass Movement shall produce all documents in its possession, custody, or control responsive to Request for Production numbers 2, 6 and 9. Within 30 days, Mass Movement shall designate and produce for deposition at Tooker & Antz, 350 Sansome Street, Suite 700, San Francisco, CA 94104 those of its officers, directors, managing agents, employees, or agents who are most qualified to testify on its behalf, to the extent of any information known or reasonably available to Mass Movement, about those matters identified in Plaintiff's Notices of Deposition of Defendant Mass Movement, Inc.'s Person Most Knowledgeable I, II, and III. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JORDAN SUSSMAN OF THE LAW FIRM OF ZIEF, DILMANT & GLICKMAN,
TEL. NO. 986-3644 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRIAN FRANKLIN OF THE ARCHER NORRIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (925) 930-6600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT MASS MOVEMENT INCORPORATED.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MASS MOVEMENT, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied in its entirety. Defendant failed to shift the burden of production. Defendant attaches the deposition of Mr. Jimenez to its moving papers and at pages 18-19 of the transcript, Mr. Jiminez indicates that defendant was the only contractor that performed installation work for 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. The acceptance doctrine does not apply to this case because the defect (lack of bolts) is fairly characterized as a latent defect. At the very least, there is a triable issue of fact regarding whether the defect was latent or patent. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jordan A. Sussman, Esq. of Zief Diment & Glickman, Tel. 415.986.3644, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Bruce C. Davis, Esq. of Prindle Amaro Goetz Hillyard Barnes & Reinholtz, Tel. 562.436.3946 x138, for defendant and moving party by court call.

RULING: The court issued its tentative ruling at the start of the hearing. The matter was argued. After hearing, DEFENDANT 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied for reasons stated on the record. Plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether plaintiff executed a release. Evidence cited in defendant's statement of undisputed material facts, the Bell Declaration and the Bell Deposition. [Prevailing party to submit a proposed order which complies with CCP 437(c) by email copying the other party.] Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
